impact: The supervisor currently treats any `[!]` checkbox as a reason to invoke repair even when the daemon has valid recovery tasks queued and an active in-progress task. Moving broad failed items out of the checkbox backlog lets the worker continue through syntax-first and fixture-first recovery tasks without losing the blocked-task context.
summary: Park broad blocked PP&D tasks as non-selectable deferred notes while keeping their narrow recovery tasks in the active checklist.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 3 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-54: Add a source freshness fixture that classifies stale PP&D guidance, changed content hashes, removed pages, and recrawl priority without performing a live recrawl.; Task checkbox-55: Add a fixture-only change report renderer for public PP&D source freshness results that summarizes changed requirements, new or removed links, and affected permit-process fixtures with citation references.; Task checkbox-59: Add validation for missing-information detector output proving every requested user item is source-backed, deduplicated, action-classified, and free of private fixture values.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
